analysis.problem: Lack of trust in the community hinders the effective response to Ebola outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient communication and engagement with local communities, leading to mistrust and skepticism about the existence and severity of the disease
leverage_points: World Health Organization (WHO) and local health authorities
Community leaders and local non-governmental organizations

proposed_actions.action: Implement community-based outreach and education programs to raise awareness about Ebola and build trust with local communities
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of community members seeking medical attention for suspected Ebola cases by 20% within the next 6 months

proposed_actions.action: Establish partnerships between local health authorities and community leaders to improve communication and collaboration
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of reported cases of violence against healthcare workers by 30% within the next 12 months

evidence: Some question if Ebola is real
winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domain of human health and dignity, as well as the broader goal of achieving sustainable development and global health security
analysis.summary: The lack of trust in the community is a significant obstacle to responding effectively to Ebola outbreaks in the DRC, and addressing this issue requires a community-based approach to build trust and improve communication

raw_items.title: Fermentation, flavour and the future of food: Making sustainability delicious
raw_items.summary:
Words like “sustainability” and “healthy” shouldn’t take the joy out of eating. As elite chef Jaume Biarnés has been explaining to UN News, sustainable gastronomy can be delicious, exciting and fun. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Fermentation, flavour and the future of food: Making sustainability delicious appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development

raw_items.title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
raw_items.summary:
Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines appeared first on

raw_items.title: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: how trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
raw_items.summary:
In Ebola-stricken Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost, humanitarians said on Tuesday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: how trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

raw_items.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
raw_items.summary:
Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday. Read Full Story on UN News
